hero.title: 5 Days — Best of North Vietnam.
hero.titleAccent: Ancient Capital, UNESCO Bay & Highland Terraces.
hero.intro: The complete North Vietnam circuit in five days. Hanoi's thousand-year-old streets. A full-day cruise through Halong Bay's UNESCO limestone karst landscape. Two nights in Sapa, with Cat Cat H'mong village and Fansipan — Vietnam's highest peak at 3,147 metres — reached by the world's longest mountain cable car. North Vietnam's three essential landscapes in a single journey.
hero.heroImageAlt: Sapa terraced rice fields H'mong village North Vietnam highlands Fansipan

quickFacts.saving: Save RM547 per person (20% online savings)
includes: Hotel accommodation (2 nights Hanoi, 2 nights Sapa)
Fansipan Sun World combo (train + cable car + funicular + buffet lunch)
Daily breakfast, lunches and dinners as specified

overview.heading: 5 Days Best of North Vietnam: Hanoi, Halong Bay & Sapa
body: This five-day package is the definitive North Vietnam highlights circuit — covering the ancient capital of Hanoi, the UNESCO karst landscape of Halong Bay, and the highland terrace country of Sapa in a single continuous journey. It is the most comprehensive five-day North Vietnam itinerary available, combining all three of the region's signature experiences without the need to separate them into different trips.

Hanoi on day one delivers the essential capital experience: the imperial mausoleum complex, the Old Quarter's 36 guild streets, Hoan Kiem Lake, and the traditional Cha Ca grilled fish that defines Hanoi's food culture. The five-hour drive to Sapa on day two follows the route past the Red River Delta farmland before climbing into the Hoang Lien Son mountain range, arriving in time for an afternoon walk to Cat Cat Village — the Black H'Mong community closest to Sapa town.

Day three is Fansipan day: the Muong Hoa Hiking Train from Sun Plaza Station to Sun World Fansipan Legend, followed by the gondola cable car to the summit station at 3,143 metres. The Great Buddha statue (21.5 metres tall, cast from 50 tons of copper) and the 11 Buddhist spiritual sites at the peak are as impressive as the mountain views. Day four brings Halong Bay: the same 2.5-hour transfer to Tuan Chau Harbour, the full day on the water with Sung Sot Cave, Luon Cave kayaking, Titov Island, and the Sunset Party on the cruise deck.

highlights.heading: Why This 5-Day North Vietnam Package Stands Apart

items.title: Fansipan — Indochina's Highest Peak
items.body: Fansipan at 3,147 metres is the highest peak in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia combined — the 'Roof of Indochina'. The Sun World Fansipan Legend complex makes the summit accessible without technical climbing: the Muong Hoa Hiking Train (a mountain rack railway) connects Sapa town to the cable car station, from where the gondola ascends 6,282 metres to the peak. The package includes the full Fansipan combo: train, cable car, funicular to the peak, buffet lunch at Van Sam Restaurant, and entrance to all 11 Buddhist sites at the summit including the 21.5-metre Great Buddha.
items.imageAlt: Fansipan peak cable car highest mountain Indochina Sapa Vietnam

items.title: Cat Cat Village — Black H'Mong Culture
items.body: Cat Cat Village is the closest traditional H'Mong settlement to Sapa town — a 30-minute walk down a well-maintained path through terraced rice paddies, past water buffaloes grazing on steep slopes, to a village where the Black H'Mong community has lived for generations. The village is named for its cascading waterfall; the women at the village gate sell indigo-dyed textiles and embroidered bags produced by hand using techniques passed down through families. The Rainbow Slide, a fun downhill slide through the village, is an optional extra.
items.imageAlt: Cat Cat Village Sapa Black H'Mong terraced rice field Vietnam

items.title: Halong Bay Full-Day Cruise
items.body: The Halong Bay day cruise covers the essential experiences of the UNESCO World Heritage karst landscape: boarding at Tuan Chau Harbour, cruising past the Dog Rock, Duck Rock, and Thumb Rock limestone formations, a buffet lunch with seafood on board, visiting Sung Sot Cave (the largest cave in the bay), kayaking at Luon Cave's enclosed lagoon, climbing Titov Island for the panoramic view, and the Sunset Party as the cruise returns through Hoa Cuong Bay. You return to Hanoi hotel in the early evening.
items.imageAlt: Halong Bay full day cruise limestone karsts UNESCO Vietnam

items.title: Hanoi City Tour — Mausoleum to Old Quarter
items.body: Hanoi's essential sights concentrate the last 1,000 years of Vietnamese history into a manageable tour: Tran Quoc Pagoda (oldest Buddhist pagoda in the city, founded in the 6th century), the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum on Ba Dinh Square (where Ho Chi Minh declared independence in 1945), Hoan Kiem Lake (the Returned Sword Lake where the Emperor Le Loi is said to have returned a magical sword to the golden turtle), the Hanoi Opera House, and the 36-guild Old Quarter. Dinner at a Cha Ca grilled fish restaurant introduces Hanoi's most distinctive dish.
items.imageAlt: Hanoi Old Quarter city tour mausoleum lake Vietnam

itinerary.heading: Your 5-Day Best of North Vietnam Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Hanoi — City Tour
days.description: Arrive at Noi Bai International Airport; hotel check-in. Guided Hanoi city tour: Tran Quoc Pagoda, Ba Dinh Square, Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, Hoan Kiem Lake, Hanoi Opera House, Old Quarter (36 Ancient Streets). En-route sample pho, banh mi, and egg coffee (Hanoi-style). Evening dinner at a Cha Ca grilled fish restaurant — turmeric-marinated fish cooked at the table with dill and spring onions.
suggestedActivities: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and Ba Dinh Square
Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple

days.title: Drive to Sapa — Cat Cat Village
days.description: 06:30–06:45: Driver picks up guests from hotel. 5.5-hour drive to Sapa (320 km via highway), with comfort breaks. Upon arrival: hotel check-in and lunch. Afternoon: guided walking tour to Cat Cat Village — a 30-minute walk from Sapa centre through rice paddy terraces to the Black H'Mong settlement. View the village's waterfall and traditional weaving. Purchase handicrafts directly from village craftswomen. Optional Rainbow Slide ride (approximately RM10, own expense). Return to hotel for freshening up. Dinner at local Sapa restaurant.
suggestedActivities: Morning drive to Sapa (5.5 hrs with breaks)

days.title: Fansipan Cable Car & Moana Sapa
days.description: 08:00: Meet guide; walk to Sapa town Sun Plaza Station. Board Muong Hoa Hiking Train to Sun World Fansipan Legend Station. Sun World combo includes: Muong Hoa train, cable car ride up to summit, entrance fees, funicular ride to Fansipan peak, and buffet lunch at Van Sam Restaurant. At the summit: 11 Buddhist spiritual sites, the 21.5-metre Great Buddha statue, and Guan Yin statue (12 metres, 18 tons). Weather permitting, views across Hoang Lien Son National Park into China. Spend approximately 4 hours at the complex. Afternoon: Moana Sapa photographic spot (Infinity Swing, Bali Gate, Golden Hand sculpture, panoramic mountain views). Dinner at own arrangement.
suggestedActivities: Muong Hoa Hiking Train to Sun World
Great Buddha statue and 11 Buddhist sites
Buffet lunch at Van Sam Restaurant
Moana Sapa — Infinity Swing, Bali Gate

days.title: Return Hanoi — Halong Bay Day Cruise
days.description: Return drive from Sapa to Hanoi (approximately 5 hours). Arrive Hanoi; 07:30–08:00 transfer to Tuan Chau Harbour (150 km, 2.5 hours). 10:00: Board cruise for Halong Bay day tour. Buffet lunch on board. Sung Sot Cave visit, Luon Cave kayaking (30 minutes), Titov Island panoramic viewpoint. Sunset Party on deck. Return to Tuan Chau Harbour 17:30–18:00; return to Hanoi hotel. Dinner at own arrangement.
suggestedActivities: Sung Sot Cave (largest cave in Halong)

days.title: Hanoi Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Hanoi for self-arranged activities. Final Old Quarter walk, Hoa Lo Prison Museum, or a last bowl of pho. Transfer to Noi Bai International Airport per your agreed schedule.

whyBook.heading: North Vietnam's Best — Arranged by Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: The Hanoi–Halong–Sapa circuit is the most popular North Vietnam itinerary we arrange for Malaysian travellers, and we have been doing it since 1988. Our guides know Sapa's hill tribe villages, Fansipan's optimal visiting times, and the Halong Bay cruise operators who maintain their vessels properly. Every logistics detail — the highway timing, the cruise boarding, the Fansipan cable car queues — is managed so you can focus on the experience.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. The Hanoi–Halong–Sapa circuit is our most booked North Vietnam itinerary.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to North Vietnam. Our guides in Hanoi, Sapa, and on Halong Bay are trusted long-term partners.

points.title: Fansipan Combo Included
points.body: The full Sun World Fansipan combo (Muong Hoa train + cable car + funicular + entrance fees + buffet lunch) is included. No separate ticketing at the mountain.

points.title: Buy 3 Free 1 Promotion
points.body: Groups of 4 pay for 3 — the 4th person travels free. Ask about this offer when booking.

sections.heading: 5 Day Best of North Vietnam Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
The 5-day Best of North Vietnam tour covers the three must-do experiences in the region — Hanoi, Halong Bay, and Sapa — without requiring separate trips. For Malaysian travellers who want to see North Vietnam's essential landscapes in a single efficient journey, this package delivers the UNESCO karst seascape, the highland rice terrace culture, and the Fansipan summit in five days, staying in a different location each night for maximum variety.

The package is particularly popular for its inclusion of Fansipan — the highest peak in Vietnam and Indochina, made accessible by the Sun World cable car system from Sapa. The combination of the mountain summit, the Black H'Mong village walk, and the Halong Bay cruise in a single 5-day circuit is unique among North Vietnam tour packages from Malaysia.

faq.heading: Questions About the 5-Day Best of North Vietnam Package

items.question: How does this package differ from the 6-day Hanoi Halong Sapa package?
items.answer: The 6-day package (pkgid 2165) gives 2 nights in Sapa with an overnight cruise on Halong Bay — more time in both destinations. This 5-day package covers the same three regions in a more compact format, using a Halong Bay day cruise rather than overnight. If you have the extra day, the 6-day package is worth the upgrade. If your schedule is tight, this 5-day package covers the essential highlights.

items.question: Is the Halong Bay experience in this package a day cruise or overnight?
items.answer: It is a full-day cruise — you board at Tuan Chau Harbour in the morning, spend the full day on the water (including Sung Sot Cave, kayaking, Titov Island, and Sunset Party), and return to Hanoi hotel in the evening. This is a day trip format, not an overnight.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. No advance application required.

items.question: Is Fansipan difficult to reach?
items.answer: No. The Sun World Fansipan Legend system makes the summit accessible without any trekking. From Sapa town, you take the Muong Hoa Hiking Train to the cable car station, the gondola up to the upper station, and the funicular the final distance to the summit at 3,143 metres. No physical fitness requirement beyond normal walking. The entire ascent takes approximately 45 minutes. Allow 4 hours at the summit to see everything.
